## Firenze: la culla del Potere di Cosimo I  ...    Firenze è indissolubilmente legata alla figura di Cosimo: la città non solo divenne il centro del suo potere politico, ma anche un laboratorio vivente delle sue ambizioni culturali e architettoniche. Palazzo Medici Riccardi, commissionato da Cosimo il Vecchio a Michelozzo intorno al 1444, fu la prima residenza ufficiale dei Medici e simbolo della loro ascesa.Quando Cosimo divenne granduca, trasferì la sede del governo a Palazzo Vecchio, trasformandolo nel Palazzo Ducale e adattandolo alle esigenze del nuovo stato toscano:  ... title: Palazzo Medici Riccardi
highlights: Medici Riccardi is a Renaissance palace in Florence, Italy, commissioned in 1444 by Cosimo de' Medici, the head of the powerful Medici banking family, and designed by architect Michelozzo di Bartolomeo as the family's first urban residence.[1] Built between 1444 and approximately 1460, it exemplifies early Renaissance architecture through its  ...    toward revived antique forms.[1][2] The palace served as the Medici headquarters during their dominance over Florentine  ...    the 19th century and now functioning as a museum and provincial administrative center.[1] Cosimo de' Medici, head of the Medici banking family, commissioned the construction of the Palazzo Medici in 1444, entrusting the design to architect Michelozzo di Bartolomeo after rejecting Filippo Brunelleschi's proposal as excessively lavish.[4] The project reflected Cosimo's strategy to project familial wealth and stability amid Florence's  ...    received fine stucco decorations between 1691 and 1692 by artisans Giovan

Palazzo Medici Riccardi in the time of Cosimo the Elder. The first Medici palace in the city The palace of Via Larga, now known as Palazzo Medici Riccardi, is one of the most important historical buildings of Florence and was once the Medici’s city residence. Realized by Michelozzo, it was commissioned in 1444 by Cosimo the Elder and was the first Medici palace in the city.
